intel.headline: Wärtsilä books 790 MW Texas order — delivery 2028-2029
intel.detail: Latest Wärtsilä data center order extends backlog past 2028. New POs now 30+ months from slot lock. This is on top of the 507 MW (Nov 2025) and 412 MW Ohio (Apr 2026) orders. The 50SG queue is effectively closed for near-term.
intel.action: Call Risto Paldanius (+1 281 233 6200) — ask specifically: 'Do you have any 2026-2027 cancellation slots?' Do not ask about new POs.

intel.headline: Wärtsilä 34SG data center debut — 412 MW Ohio (40 engines)
intel.detail: 34SG (10.3 MW each) just made its first data center appearance. Separate production line from 50SG — may have shorter backlog. Ohio project = 40 engines at 10.3 MW each.
intel.action: Ask Risto specifically about 34SG lead time vs 50SG. Could be 6-12 months faster.

intel.headline: Frame turbine lead times hit 243 weeks (4.7 yrs) — Wood Mackenzie
intel.detail: Some frame turbine classes now 5-7 years. Reciprocating engines (Wärtsilä, INNIO) faster at 18-30 months but still backlogged. BTM gas is the data center industry's primary strategy — 56% of developers pursuing it. Your multi-source approach is exactly right.
intel.action: Every week without a PO placed is a week pushed back. Tier 1 used/surplus units are now more valuable than ever — pursue aggressively.

intel.headline: Solar Mars 100 — 10 MW x2 — overhauled — Surplus Record
intel.detail: Two overhauled Solar Mars 100 Version 15000SAN. 10 MW each. 11,500V, 60 Hz, ISO-rated, natural gas. DOM 2011, warranty expired, sold as-is. Combined: 20 MW in a single transaction.
intel.action: Call Surplus Record immediately. Two 10 MW units is 20% of Phase 1 target. Request logbook, overhaul records, and inspection access.

intel.headline: Solar Taurus 60 — 5.2 MW — trailer-mounted — Surplus Record
intel.detail: Solar Taurus 60, TG01827, 5.2 MW, 13.8 kV, 60 Hz, natural gas. 33,540 hours since new. Includes trailer chassis — fully mobile. Deploy to any site without a foundation.
intel.action: 33,540 hrs is high but Taurus 60s are workhorses. Mobile unit = deploy to Trappeys or any ADC site. Request full inspection.

intel.headline: ProEnergy PE6000 — 48 MW/unit — 2027 delivery confirmed
intel.detail: PE6000 = repurposed CF6-80C2 Boeing 747 core, rebuilt as 48 MW aero turbine. 5-min start, 72-hr swap. Landon Tessmer confirmed 2027 new-build slots available. 75 units fabricated since 2020; 52 on order. Two units = 96 MW.
intel.action: Call Landon Tessmer at ProEnergy (proenergyservices.com). Two PE6000s covers 96 MW. This is the fastest path to 100 MW that still has open slots.

intel.headline: VoltaGrid locked — Oracle 2.3 GW + INNIO 1.5 GW order
intel.detail: VoltaGrid signed 2.3 GW deal with Oracle for Texas AI data centers, first units operational April 2026. Also holds 1.5 GW INNIO order (300 Jenbacher engines). Bridge power capacity heavily committed. Louisiana availability unlikely near-term.
intel.action: Send bridge RFI as a placeholder relationship-builder but do not put VoltaGrid on the critical path.

opportunity_leads.notes: Two overhauled units. DOM 2011. 11,500V 60 Hz ISO-rated. Warranty expired, as-is. Call immediately — 20 MW in one transaction.
33,540 hrs since new. 13.8 kV, 60 Hz, natural gas. Trailer chassis included. Mobile deployment — no foundation needed.

opportunity_leads.contact: Landon Tessmer (VP Comm Ops) / Jeff Canon (CEO) / proenergyservices.com
opportunity_leads.notes: 2027 delivery confirmed. 48 MW each, 5-min start, 72-hr hot-swap. Repurposed Boeing 747 CF6-80C2 cores. 75 built since 2020. FASTEST path to 100 MW with open slots.

opportunity_leads.contact: Risto Paldanius / +1 281 233 6200 / 11710 N. Gessner Rd Suite A, Houston TX 77064
opportunity_leads.notes: 790 MW Texas order April 2026, delivery 2028-2029. New POs at 30+ months. Ask only about cancellation slots in 2026-2027 delivery schedule. Do not ask about new orders.
34SG just made data center debut (412 MW Ohio, Apr 2026). May have shorter backlog than 50SG — separate production. Ask Risto directly: 34SG vs 50SG lead time difference.
10.6 MW per unit, 48.7% electrical efficiency, 2-min start. VoltaGrid holds 1.5 GW INNIO order (300 engines) — production line very tight. Contact NA office directly for independent lead time quote.

opportunity_leads.contact: Nathan Ough (CEO) / David Bell (VP DC Dev) / voltagrid.com
opportunity_leads.notes: Oracle 2.3 GW + INNIO 1.5 GW order means capacity is spoken for. Send PPA/bridge RFI for Louisiana anyway — relationship placeholder. Do not put on critical path.
GE, Siemens, Solar, Cat, Wärtsilä available in 1-9 MW class. Inventory turns weekly. Check online listing page every Monday and after any news of plant retirements.
